title: BS Intro
content:
Bootstrap Introduction

What is Bootstrap?

Bootstrap is the most popular CSS framework for building responsive, mobile-first websites. It provides pre-built CSS classes and JavaScript components that make web development faster and easier.

Why Use Bootstrap?

  • Responsive: Works seamlessly on all devices (phones, tablets, desktops).
  • Easy to Use: Pre-built components save hours of coding.
  • Browser Compatible: Works across all modern browsers.

Getting Started

Include Bootstrap via CDN in your HTML <head>:

Bootstrap CDN
<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.0/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css" rel="stylesheet">

title: BS Containers
content:
Bootstrap Containers

The Foundation

Containers are the most basic layout element in Bootstrap and are required when using the default grid system. They pad the content inside them.



Container Types

Container Example
<!-- Provides a responsive fixed width container -->
<div class="container">Fixed Container</div>

<!-- Provides a full width container, spanning the entire width of the viewport -->
<div class="container-fluid">Full Width Container</div>

title: BS Grid System
content:
Bootstrap Grid System

How the Grid Works

Bootstrap's grid system allows up to 12 columns across the page. If you do not want to use all 12 columns individually, you can group the columns together to create wider columns.



Grid Example

Basic Grid
<div class="row">
  <div class="col">Column 1</div>
  <div class="col">Column 2</div>
  <div class="col">Column 3</div>
</div>

title: BS Columns
content:
Bootstrap Columns

Column Sizing

You can manually specify how many of the 12 grid columns each element should span by adding a number to the class name.

Column Examples

Column Classes
<div class="row">
  <!-- 4 + 4 + 4 = 12 total columns -->
  <div class="col-4">.col-4</div>
  <div class="col-4">.col-4</div>
  <div class="col-4">.col-4</div>
</div>

title: BS Typography
content:
Bootstrap Typography

Headings & Display

Bootstrap provides beautifully styled heading tags by default. It also offers "Display" classes to make headings stand out even more with a larger font size and lighter font weight.

Typography Examples

Headings
<h1>Heading 1</h1>
<h2>Heading 2</h2>

<!-- Display headings are larger and lighter -->
<h1 class="display-1">Display 1</h1>

title: BS Colors
content:
Bootstrap Colors

Contextual Colors

Bootstrap has semantic, contextual color classes to convey meaning through colors for text and backgrounds.



Color Examples

Text Colors
<!-- Usually blue -->
<p class="text-primary">.text-primary</p>

<!-- Usually green -->
<p class="text-success">.text-success</p>

<!-- Usually red -->
<p class="text-danger">.text-danger</p>

title: BS Buttons
content:
Bootstrap Buttons

Button Classes

Bootstrap provides various button styles to indicate different actions or states.

Button Examples

Basic Buttons
<button class="btn btn-primary">Primary</button>
<button class="btn btn-secondary">Secondary</button>
<button class="btn btn-success">Success</button>
<button class="btn btn-danger">Danger</button>

title: BS Cards
content:
Bootstrap Cards

What are Cards?

Cards are flexible, extensible content containers with multiple variants and options.



Basic Card

Card Example
<div class="card" style="width: 300px;">
  <div class="card-body">
    <h5 class="card-title">Card Title</h5>
    <p class="card-text">Card content goes here.</p>
  </div>
</div>

title: BS Forms
content:
Bootstrap Forms

Form Controls

Bootstrap form controls receive global styling with the .form-control class, instantly making them look modern and responsive.



Form Example

Basic Form
<form>
  <div class="mb-3">
    <label class="form-label">Email</label>
    <input type="email" class="form-control">
  </div>
  <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Submit</button>
</form>

title: BS Tables
content:
Bootstrap Tables

Basic Table

Add the .table class to any standard HTML table to instantly apply Bootstrap's clean padding and horizontal dividers.

Table Example

Basic Table
<table class="table">
  <thead>
    <tr><th>#</th><th>First Name</th></tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr><td>1</td><td>Mark</td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

title: BS Alerts
content:
Bootstrap Alerts

Alert Messages

Alerts are available for any length of text and are perfect for showing success, warning, or error messages to the user.

Alert Examples

Basic Alerts
<div class="alert alert-primary">Primary Alert</div>
<div class="alert alert-success">Success Alert</div>
<div class="alert alert-danger">Danger Alert</div>

title: BS Badges
content:
Bootstrap Badges

What are Badges?

Badges are small count and labeling components used to highlight new items or notification counts.

Badge Examples

Basic Badges
<h1>Heading <span class="badge bg-primary">New</span></h1>
<button class="btn btn-primary">
  Notifications <span class="badge bg-secondary">4</span>
</button>

title: BS Progress
content:
Bootstrap Progress Bars

Basic Progress

Progress components are built with two HTML elements and a bit of inline CSS to set the width.

Progress Examples

Basic Progress
<div class="progress">
  <div class="progress-bar" style="width: 25%">25%</div>
</div>

title: BS Spinners
content:
Bootstrap Spinners

Loading Spinners

Spinners show the loading state in your projects. They are built entirely with HTML and CSS.

Spinner Examples

Basic Spinners
<div class="spinner-border"></div>
<div class="spinner-border text-primary"></div>

title: BS Navbar
content:
Bootstrap Navigation Bar

What is a Navbar?

The navbar is a responsive navigation header that automatically collapses on smaller screens and provides a hamburger menu.



Navbar Example

Basic Navbar
<nav class="navbar navbar-expand-lg navbar-light bg-light">
  <a class="navbar-brand" href="#">Navbar</a>
</nav>

title: BS Navs
content:
Bootstrap Navigation

Nav Components

Bootstrap's nav provides a simple base for building navigation elements like tabs and pills.

Nav Tabs

Tabs Example
<ul class="nav nav-tabs">
  <li class="nav-item"><a class="nav-link active" href="#">Active</a></li>
  <li class="nav-item"><a class="nav-link" href="#">Link</a></li>
</ul>

title: BS Modals
content:
Bootstrap Modals

What are Modals?

Modals are dialog boxes or popups that appear on top of the current page, dimming the background to focus the user's attention.



Modal Example

Basic Modal
<button class="btn btn-primary" data-bs-toggle="modal" data-bs-target="#myModal">Open Modal</button>

<div class="modal" id="myModal">
  <div class="modal-dialog">
    <div class="modal-content">
      <div class="modal-body">Modal Content Goes Here</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

title: BS Carousel
content:
Bootstrap Carousel

What is a Carousel?

The carousel is a slideshow component for cycling through elements, typically images or text cards.



Carousel Example

Image Slider
<div class="carousel slide" data-bs-ride="carousel">
  <div class="carousel-inner">
    <div class="carousel-item active">Slide 1</div>
    <div class="carousel-item">Slide 2</div>
  </div>
</div>

title: BS Utilities
content:
Bootstrap Utilities

Spacing Utilities

Bootstrap includes dozens of shorthand utility classes for rapidly adjusting margin and padding without writing custom CSS.

Spacing Examples

Spacing Classes
<div class="mt-4">Margin Top 4</div>
<div class="p-5">Padding 5</div>
<div class="mx-auto">Centered horizontally</div>

title: BS Borders
content:
Bootstrap Borders

Border Utilities

Use border utilities to quickly style the border and border-radius of an element.

Border Examples

Basic Borders
<span class="border"></span>
<span class="border border-primary rounded"></span>

title: BS Flexbox
content:
Bootstrap Flexbox

Flexbox Utilities

Turn on flexbox mode with flex utilities to quickly manage the layout, alignment, and sizing of grid columns, navigation, components, and more.


[Image of CSS Flexbox layout model showing container and items]

Flex Examples

Flex Container
<div class="d-flex">Flexbox Container</div>
<div class="d-inline-flex">Inline Flexbox</div>

title: BS Images
content:
Bootstrap Images

Responsive Images

Images in Bootstrap are made responsive with .img-fluid. This applies max-width: 100%; and height: auto; to the image so that it scales with the parent element.

Image Examples

Responsive Image
<!-- Scales gracefully to the parent container -->
<img src="https://picsum.photos/800/400" class="img-fluid" alt="...">

<!-- Adds a neat 1px border rounded thumbnail look -->
<img src="https://picsum.photos/200" class="img-thumbnail" alt="...">

title: BS Icons
content:
Bootstrap Icons

Using Icons

Bootstrap Icons is a library of over 1,500 high-quality, open-source icons. You can include them easily via CDN.

Icon Examples

Include Icons
<!-- Include the CDN in your document head -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap-icons@1.10.0/font/bootstrap-icons.css">

<!-- Use the icons anywhere! -->
<i class="bi bi-alarm"></i>
<button class="btn btn-primary"><i class="bi bi-airplane"></i> Fly</button>

title: BS Responsive
content:
Bootstrap Responsive Design

Responsive Classes

Bootstrap provides responsive classes for showing and hiding elements based on the screen size (breakpoints like sm, md, lg, xl).


[Image of responsive web design breakpoints showing mobile tablet and desktop layouts]

Display Utilities

Show/Hide
<div class="d-none">Hidden on all screens</div>
<div class="d-block">Visible as a block element</div>

<!-- Hidden on mobile, but becomes visible on medium (tablet) screens and up! -->
<div class="d-none d-md-block">Visible on md+</div>

title: BS Components
content:
Bootstrap Components

List Groups

List groups are a flexible and powerful component for displaying a series of content. Modify and extend them to support just about any content within.

List Group Example

Basic List Group
<ul class="list-group">
  <li class="list-group-item">First item</li>
  <li class="list-group-item">Second item</li>
</ul>

title: BS Course Summary
content:
Bootstrap Complete Course Summary 🚀

You Did It! 🏆

Congratulations! You have successfully completed the Bootstrap course. You now have the skills to build beautiful, responsive, mobile-first websites in record time.

  • Layout: Mastered the 12-column Grid System and flexible Containers.
  • Components: Styled interactive Buttons, Cards, Forms, and Navbars.
  • Utilities: Harnessed Spacing classes, Colors, and advanced Flexbox logic.

Keep building amazing, responsive websites!
